source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
If you add the numbers on opposite faces of this special die, you will get the same total three times. The numbers on the hidden faces of the die are prime numbers. Which number is on the face opposite to 14?
<image1>
extraction_notes: Used raw_record.image as the image path.

image_dependency: requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the problem is primarily visual
Question refers to 'this special die' and asks about opposite/hidden faces, which requires the die image layout
The needed face values are not fully specified in text; the visible die labels must be read from the image
multi_step: Requires combining at least two constraints: opposite faces sum to the same total, and hidden faces are prime numbers
Likely needs visual extraction of visible face numbers from the die image before solving for the unseen opposite face
Target asks for the number opposite to 14, so the solver must infer a common opposite-face sum and then determine the matching hidden value
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single explicit number ('23')
The puzzle constraints are structured and checkable: opposite-face sums and primality of hidden faces can be validated against the image
asset_registry_record shows registry_passed=true, image exists, and answer is present

decision_reasons: image, question, and numeric answer are all present, so the core semantics are available
asset registry passed with no recorded issues
high image_dependency, multi_step, and verifiability scores indicate a strong candidate
although visual details are not fully textualized, the required image asset exists, so this is not a blocking gap
